steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: The researchers concluded that the trait's prevalence—the percentage of species displaying it—varies systematically with absolute latitude across all avian orders, making latitude the key factor determining how common the trait is.

steps.title: Find evidence of the latitude-prevalence relationship
steps.content:
C directly demonstrates this: prevalence increases with latitude, from 34% at 0-30° to 60% at 50-80° absolute latitude across orders. A contradicts the finding by claiming latitude doesn't affect Charadriiforms, B fails to show clear prevalence rates, and D shows only trait distribution by location rather than prevalence variation. This validates the researchers' conclusion that latitude mediates the trait's prevalence across orders.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
